programme.title: The Detectives

programme.desc: A detailed look at a famed, iconic television show from Canada that impacted multiple generations and its ongoing legacy.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: Forests are essential for life on our planet, but humans are cutting more down every year; these inspiring people are reshaping how we live and work in Earth's forests, sharing space with wildlife.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: The wild horse has been saved from extinction, resulting in over 400 breeds.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: There's still so much not known about fear; a look at whether fear is good for people and what life would be like without it.

programme.title: The Killer Within
programme.desc: Looking at DNA evidence that allowed detectives to nab a killer many years later.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: Frustrated parents say they should be able to force their opioid-addicted kids into care; in B.C., the province with the highest rate of opioid deaths, the laws say kids can deny care and insist doctors don't tell their parents if they are hooked.

programme.title: Wheeler's Everest
programme.desc: Oliver Wheeler's great-grandson Jeff Wheeler and explorer and author Wade Davis travel in Oliver's footsteps, retracing that first expedition and rediscovering adventures and achievements.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director Martin Scorsese, whose films include Taxi Driver, Raging Bull and Goodfellas and who won an Oscar for directing The Departed.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director Oliver Stone, whose films include JFK, Nixon and Wall Street and who won Oscars for Platoon and Born on the Fourth of July.

programme.title: Man on Wire
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge.

programme.title: I Am Joe Frazier
programme.desc: A profile of the former world heavyweight boxing champion, a perfectionist who triumphantly emerged from Muhammad Ali's long shadow.

programme.title: JFK Revisited: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's documentary examining the assassination of US president John F Kennedy, using James DiEugenio's 1992 book Destiny Betrayed: JFK, Cuba and the Garrison Case, and recently declassified US government files to shine a light on what happened in Dallas on November 22, 1963. Narrated by Whoopi Goldberg and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: Chernobyl: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: Newly discovered archive footage and witness accounts reveal the story behind the 1986 nuclear disaster and the heroic efforts made to prevent another explosion.

programme.title: Rik Mayall: Magnificent B'stard
programme.desc: A documentary tribute to the influential and much-missed comedian, a man driven by punk spirit and an unrelenting commitment to pushing boundaries. programme.title: Katie Price: Nothing to Hide
programme.desc: A profile of one of Britain's most enduring celebrity figures, combining previously unseen footage with first-hand testimonies from Katie and those closest to her.

programme.title: Tina
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome.

programme.title: Vegas: The Story of Sin City
programme.desc: A look at how Las Vegas became farther removed from national attitudes, with Elvis Presley's comeback helping cement the city as an international entertainment destination.

programme.title: Vegas: The Story of Sin City
programme.desc: How the Strip exploded with new Disney-fied hotels, casinos and megastars, highlighting its evolution and continual reinvention in the face of difficulty.

programme.title: Steve McQueen: The Lost Movie
programme.desc: Documentary exploring a movie Steve McQueen began work on in the 1960s, but which was never completed.

programme.title: Red Elvis: The Cold War Cowboy
programme.desc: Documentary about an American pop star who defected to the Soviet Bloc in the 1970s, becoming a Marxist icon in Eastern Europe until his mysterious drowning in East Berlin.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Steven Spielberg, whose films include Jaws, ET the Extra-Terrestrial, Raiders of the Lost Ark and Jurassic Park and who won Oscars for directing Schindler's List and Saving Private Ryan.

programme.title: The Eighties

programme.desc: The life and career of the American actor, who won an Oscar for his role in The Fugitive and also starred in The Executioner's Song, Men in Black, JFK and No Country for Old Men.

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The journey through the history of American cinema concludes with a look at cinema from the 2000s through to the present day.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: An account of the origins of the American Civil War, which began in 1861 when 11 southern slave states formed the Confederate States of America and declared their withdrawal from the United States. Abraham Lincoln and Jefferson Davis vied as presidents of the Union and Confederacy respectively.

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at the development of situation comedies, tracing their evolution from the 1950s to the 21st century, beginning by looking at the depictions of families.

programme.title: Laurel and Hardy: Their Lives and Magic
programme.desc: Documentary shedding new light on the turbulent private lives of Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy and the genuine friendship between them.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.title: My Icon: Solomon Wilcots
programme.desc: The former NFL player talks about his idol.

programme.title: The Alpinist
programme.desc: Documentary about elusive Canadian climber Marc-André Leclerc, who has made some of the boldest solo ascents in history, away from cameras and with no margin for error.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: My Best Friend's an Animal
programme.desc: Animals that have unlikely friendships with humans, beginning with Sirga the lion, whose human pal, Val, has been by her side since she was an orphaned cub.

programme.title: My Best Friend's an Animal

programme.desc: The northern team attach an activity tag to a bull shark, before diving 80 metres deep to put a satellite tracker on a great white.

programme.desc: A team studies how young blacktips learn social and survival skills, while another tries to catch the sand tiger shark's most elusive behaviour - mating.

programme.desc: How volcanos have altered the planet. Volcanologist Tom Pfeiffer takes a group to Ertea Ale. However, first they need to cross Ethiopia's Danakil Depression - one of the planet's most inhospitable places.

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys
programme.desc: Volcanologist Tom Pfeiffer heads to Indonesia to see if Anak, a son of legendary volcano Krakatoa, is ready to repeat the pattern of its ancestor.

programme.title: Extreme Africa

programme.title: Extreme Africa

programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. programme.title: Wild Survivors

programme.title: Wild Survivors
programme.desc: It is the dry season in the Luangwa Valley and for herbivores food is hard to come by, but one animal's misfortune can be another's good luck.

programme.title: Wild Survivors
programme.desc: A look at how Zambia's Luangwa Valley is in constant flux, influenced by the myriad of animals that call it home.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The powers of fire and ice collide in Iceland, actually Earth's largest volcanic island. Take a journey to this country driven by the forces of nature.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas

programme.title: Nature's Dress Code: Who Wears What and Why?
programme.desc: Every living creature, from the amoeba to the blue whale, needs a suitable coat. Dive deep into the fashion styles of the natural world with this colourful documentary.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: When a tiny piece of trash ruins a raccoon's life, Doctor Jessica cuts it free - but Hope faces tough decisions about its future.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset welcomes its first saki monkey and the park's vets struggle to fathom what is bothering ring-tailed lemur Houdini.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Jethro battles with thieving squirrel monkeys and orangutan Hsiao-Quai takes her adopted daughter outside for the first time. Plus, staff suspect a woolly monkey could be pregnant.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: This edition celebrates the new Orangutan Jungle School graduates and cameras follow the convoy to the release site where the team bids their friends a poignant farewell.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A retired military couple wants to move from Maryland to find a forever home n Michigan's Duck Lake. They're willing to put in some sweat equity for renovations with hopes they can inspire their children and grandchildren to make frequent visits.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys create a rustic family gathering place in Franklin, Tenn. for a homeowner who loves 19th century hand-hewed craftsmanship; the property sits on a former horse farm that now serves as a dog rehabilitation camp.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys revive a 19th century cabin taken down in Ripley, W.Va; for a break, they go to the state fair; they go to the Barnwood Living backlot, where they're challenged to make a 30-foot timber frame enclosure using only spare parts.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe looks back on some examples of handcrafted stonework; along the way, he and the guys find fossils and arrowheads, explore a coal mine and skip rocks.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple from Virginia Beach search the laid back community of Kill Devil Hills, NC, where they hope to find a beach house they can renovate and rent.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Connecticut house flipper is on the hunt for a property with a beach view that he can call his own; after years of renovating houses for others to use, he is ready to find a vacation home that he and his family can escape to for some quality time.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A husband and wife house-flipping team decide to renovate a home in Okaloosa County, Fla., putting their skills and patience to the test as they try to turn their property into a dream home for buyers.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple living in Seattle searches for a vacation getaway along the sands of Ocean Park, Wash, and waterfront homes in this Pacific coast community aren't cheap, so finding a deal could require some renovations.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Carolina family finds a deal on their new beach house on Pawleys Island, S.C.; a massive overhaul of the floor plan and expansions on both floors guarantees room for their growing family.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple from Washington, DC searches the shores of Maryland's Chesapeake Bay for a bargain beach house that they can make their own; a problem uncovered during demolition threatens to increase their already tightened renovation budget.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple inherit a quaint cabin; Chase and his team focus on transforming the former fish and lobster shack into a fun family cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: An early 1900s cabin that's intimately tied to Maine's history and legacy puts the team to the test as they try to re-create its original look and feel.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the crew are tasked with the relocation and land manifestation of a yurt on an island on Togus Pond; the team seeks to beat the ice and snow so they can have the yurt ready for the family to drop their fishing lines in the frozen pond.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A man and his fiancé would like to see their rustic camping cabin without plumbing transformed into a place they can entertain, use the bathroom indoors and enjoy multiple seasons; they'd also like to pay homage to their funeral home business.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate an off-the-grid camp for a family of city slickers; with limited resources, the team members work to incorporate the comforts of city living, then they discover a moldering water tank that may shut down the renovation.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A woman looks for a home on North Carolina's Oak Island to escape the cold New Hampshire winters; with the help of her construction savvy dad, she renovates a house into the beach home of her dreams for her and her family to enjoy for years to come.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Willing to put in the renovation work themselves, a young couple expecting their first child searches for an outdated house to transform into a forever home--for a fraction of the price of new waterfront properties in Bristol, Rhode Island.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Utah couple visited Oak Island and loved it so much they moved to North Carolina; after years of outings to the island, they're ready to buy and renovate their own beach home where their two young kids can grow up experiencing beach life.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple who flips homes uses all their experience to buy and renovate an outdated beach house straight out of the 1970s; they put in plenty of sweat equity to keep the costs low and get the home ready for the next family of buyers.

programme.desc: First-time homeowners from the Jersey shore are looking to escape the cold and find their piece of paradise in Madeira Beach, Fla.; they're hoping to find a home with water views that they can renovate as a place to raise their family.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys travel to West Alexander, Pa., the heart of farm country, to save a classic bank barn; Johnny visits a local farmer and her pasture-raised animals; Mark drops by a local farmers' market to buy goodies for the crew.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe and his designer Karen Tillery take steps to realize his dream of building a small village of finished antique structures in White Sulphur Springs, West Virginia.
